title: Kafka Dynamic Broker Configuration Change Detection
spl:
index=kafka sourcetype="kafka:serverLog"
| search "Dynamic" AND ("config" OR "configuration") AND ("update" OR "alter" OR "changed")
| rex "^\[[^\]]+\]\s+(?<log_level>\w+)"
| where log_level IN ("INFO","WARN","ERROR")
| table _time, host, log_level, _raw
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Broker logs emit entries when `dynamicConfig` or alter-config paths change retention, ACLs, or listener properties—events that should map to approved change tickets.
value: Speeds incident correlation when a sudden behavior change traces to an unplanned broker property update.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to speed incident correlation when a sudden behavior change traces to an unplanned broker property update.
implementation: Tune keywords to your broker distribution (Apache vs Confluent). Feed results into a change correlation lookup (ticket ID in CMDB). Suppress maintenance windows.
visualization: Timeline (config change events), Table (host, raw line), Single value (changes per day).

references.title: Source types for the Splunk Add-on for Kafka

knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
